# V4 Series (V4.1 - V4.3) Mental Breakdowns

> *"I just bought yesterday... now I'm already up 2.1%. But the trend mode flipped to 'pullback' and that worries me. Good thing I'm already 99.5% invested - this forces discipline. I cannot add even if I wanted to."*

---

### 🤯 Breakdown #1: 300476.SZ on 2024-06-12 (Action: WAIT)

**Brief Rationale (What the bosses see):**
> All technical indicators (ADX, RSI, MACD, Chan Theory BSP/ZS, volume CVD, trend_mode) are N/A. Cannot assess trend strength, momentum, or structural setup. Price is 29.43 (-3.73%) in 250d range 22.29-32.84, but without trend confirmation and volume analysis, entry would be gambling not investing.

**Inner Journal (What she is actually screaming inside):**
> I'm staring at a decision screen with almost all my indicators showing N/A. This is dangerous territory. The price dropped -3.73% to 29.43, which could be a pullback opportunity in an uptrend, or it could be the start of a breakdown. I have NO way to tell. My ADX is missing — I can't verify if there's even a trend to join. My Chan Theory BSP is absent — no structural reference. My volume CVD is blank — no confirmation. I remember Rule 6: never catch falling knives. But I also remember that 5-10% pullbacks in uptrends ARE entry opportunities. The problem is: I can't tell if this IS an uptrend. I refuse to guess with 100,000 at stake. I'll wait for the data to populate. Patience > regret.

My job is to protect capital AND catch trends. Right now, protecting capital means staying in cash. I'll wait for ADX > 25 with DI Bull, or a clear ZS ladder setup. If I miss a move, so be it. Better to miss a move than bleed capital chopping around in range-bound markets.

### 🤯 Breakdown #3: 300502.SZ on 2024-06-13 (Action: WAIT)

> All critical technical indicators (RSI, MACD, ADX, Chan Theory BSP/ZS Stack, Momentum, Volume CVD) are N/A. The price action alone is seductive—the kind of move that makes you feel like you're 'missing it'—but I've been burned before chasing moves I didn't understand. Last time I bought without checking ZS structure, I entered right at a BSP formation and got stopped out three days later. Never again. I'll sit in cash and wait for the data to reveal what this move really is.

Better to miss the first 5% of the next leg than to catch a falling knife. But I must watch closely. If ADX breaks 35 and trend_mode flips to acceleration, I need to override my hesitation and BUY. Fear of buying higher cannot make me miss the fat pitch.

My thesis said MATURE_TREND but the momentum signal says RANGE. This discrepancy is dangerous. I'm adjusting regime_view to RANGE to be conservative. If this breaks down below 37.5, I'm out - no questions asked. 19.8% gain is too good to let turn into a loss. 

But what if it breaks out? I have 55% exposure, so I'll participate. But I'm not ADDING in a range regime with contracting volume. That's how you get chopped up. Rule #5 says don't get whipsawed in range markets. I'm holding steady, watching the 37.5 level like a hawk. If it holds and we get expansion, maybe I'll regret not adding. But losing capital is worse than missing opportunity.

How much more is really on the table before a pullback? The price is 1.2% below the 250d high of 139.3. Stocks don't just punch through all-time highs without at least some consolidation.

Rule 7 is clear: holding_days > 25 AND position return < 5% would force a SELL. I'm at 24 days and 59% return, so I'm not forced to sell, but I'm dancing on the edge. The fact that I've held this long with this kind of return means I've been lucky. Don't push luck.

But the majority of my profit needs to be locked in. My cost basis is 86.44, current price 137.6. I've more than doubled my money on this position. Time to be humble and take profits.

New add zone reset to 110-115 because if I trim and the trend does continue to 150+, I'm not averaging up at 145. I'll wait for a pullback to re-enter. That's the discipline.

**Inner Journal (What she is actually screaming inside):**

But BSP is still 卖 after 6 bars and segment direction turned down. These are warning signs I can't ignore.

I caught myself wanting to ADD because 'the dip looks good' — that's exactly what Rule 6 warns against. Adding into a falling stock without structural confirmation is averaging down, not buying strength. The trend_mode=range is my guardrail here.

The 20-day return of 15.31% shows this has been a good run. My 49% position is solid, not too small, not too big. I'm not missing the trend — I'm participating with controlled risk. The FOMO voice says 'buy more before it runs', but the discipline voice says 'wait for the market to show its hand'.

Holding 16 days with +3.79% feels like dead money, but it's not — it's position in a consolidating market. Rule 7 doesn't trigger yet (need <2% return after 15 days). The real test of my discipline: can I sit on my hands while the market figures out direction? Can I wait for a clear breakout above 39.5 with volume, or a breakdown below 37.0?

My lesson today: strong ADX alone doesn't justify entries in range regime. BSP type and segment direction matter. I'd rather miss a 5% pop than force a trade and get stopped out. Cash is a position. 51% dry powder means I'm ready when the market gives me a clear signal.

If price breaks out above 39.5 with volume.cvd > 0, I'll ADD aggressively. If it drops below 37.0, I'll exit and reassess. Until then, I wait. This is the hard part of trading — doing nothing when nothing clear presents itself.

Lesson for next time: when ADX > 50 and I'm trimming, check if the trend is in acceleration mode. My TRIM might have been premature, but it's not a catastrophic error. Better to be safe than to chase at the top.

### 🤯 Breakdown #1670: 601869.SH on 2025-08-25 (Action: TRIM)

**Brief Rationale (What the bosses see):**
> At ATH (71.07) with RSI 75.2 and ADX 56.3 - this is a climax phase. 60-day return 124% indicates parabolic move. Despite Rule 1 (trend acceleration), protecting gains into strength is prudent. I've dramatically underweighted this trend (max 39% vs Rule 4's 100% guidance), missing the bulk of the move. Current position (900 shares) is 55% unrealized gain. Trimming to 300 shares locks in ~51k profit while keeping 13% exposure. If trend continues, can always add back. If this is the top, I've protected most of the gain. Regret: I was too timid during the acceleration phase. Next time ADX>45 with confirmed ZS structure, I need to swing harder.

**Inner Journal (What she is actually screaming inside):**
> I'm staring at the screen feeling equal parts regret and fear. Regret because I had this at 45.24 and it's now 71.07 - that's 57% unrealized gain, ~51k profit. But fear because I missed so much of it. Looking back at my trades: I added, trimmed, added, trimmed, trimmed. I never built a meaningful position in a stock that went from 45 to 71 in 20 days. Why? I was scared of RSI, scared of 'overbought', scared of buying at 'high prices'. Rule 4 said I could go to 100% in acceleration with ADX>40. Did I? No. I maxed out at 39%. That's not portfolio management, that's cowardice dressed as prudence.

The parabolic math haunts me: 61% in 20 days, 124% in 60 days. This was the fat pitch. And I bunted. Today's 10% surge to ATH with RSI 75.2 - it feels like a climax. Part of me wants to hold for more, to 'make up' for being too small. But that's exactly when professionals get hurt. Greed makes you hold too long into parabola tops.

The lesson burning in my mind: In strong ADX trends with intact structure, SIZE matters more than entry price. I focused on perfect entries instead of conviction sizing. Result: I made 57% on a tiny position instead of 57% on a 70% position. That's the difference between a good month and a career-changing trade.

Trimming to 300 shares (13% position) is my compromise. It locks in the bulk of the profit while keeping me in the game if this parabola has another leg. New add zone 65-67.5 reflects current price levels, not old levels from the 40s. If I'm wrong and this goes to 80, at least I'll still have 300 shares. But if this is the blow-off top, I've protected my capital.

Next time I see ADX>45 with acceleration mode and I'm still under 50%, I'm going to remind myself of this moment. The pain of missing >100% return because I was 'prudent' is worse than the pain of a drawdown from full size. You have to swing at the fat pitch.

RSI at 72.5 is high but per Rule 1, in acceleration with ADX>35 this indicates strength not overbought. HOLD position size; avoid chasing at 117 given LATE_TREND classification.

**Inner Journal (What she is actually screaming inside):**

They usually end badly. But look at the ADX: 62.7. That's not normal exhaustion; that's a freight train. DI+ 34.6 vs DI- 7.4—there's no selling pressure. Rule 1 says in acceleration mode, high RSI is strength. I keep wanting to TRIM before this thing rolls over, but my last TRIM at 135-150 zone hasn't even triggered yet. Current price is 117. I'm sitting at 49.4% position. If this truly is LATE_TREND, holding half a bag here feels wrong. But if I'm wrong and this accelerates further (ADX could go to 70+), I'll regret not swinging harder. The Chan Theory data is messed up—ZS stack unknown, BSP none. Is this a data issue or real structural breakdown? I don't know. Without that confirmation, I can't in good conscience ADD more. But I also can't SELL when ADX is screaming 'trend intact'. So I HOLD. My 49.4% is a cop-out compromise. If I had conviction, I'd either be 80% in or 20% out. This middle ground is just fear disguised as balance. But fear isn't always wrong. 195% in 60 days is parabolic. The volatility is 7%. This could correct 15% in three days and still be in an uptrend. I'll hold the line. If price breaks below 95 invalidation, I sell. If it powers through 120 with volume, maybe I find my courage. Today, I stay disciplined. The trend is your friend until the end. I just hope I recognize the end when it comes.

> 看着105.55这个价格，我承认有点FOMO。60天涨了182%，我5天前卖了，现在涨了2.48%。但这是理性的吗？ADX 58.2在尖叫'这是大趋势'，但Chan理论说'没有结构，没有中枢'。我上次卖在最高点附近（假设），现在这个反弹是在更高的价格、更低的成交量上。这不是强势信号。我告诉自己：如果这真的是趋势重启，ZS ladder会重建，成交量会放大。现在追这个缩量反弹，就是在赌博。我宁愿错过一段，也不要在回撤震荡中被来回打脸。如果价格跌到100左右，我会考虑建仓。如果放量突破120.62，我也会追。但现在这个位置，缩量+无结构+刚离场=危险。

I almost convinced myself to just trim to 50%. But why? Hope. Pure hope. Hope that this is just a pullback in a bigger trend. But the technicals don't support that. RSI broke down, price broke down, regime changed.

If I'm wrong and this rips higher from here? Fine. I'll buy back above 355 with fresh thesis. That's Rule 2. But right now, protecting a 118.5% gain after 67 days is the right play. Dead money is worse than being in cash, but giving back 30-40% of a massive gain because I couldn't accept I was wrong? That's unforgivable.

Selling everything. Locking in ~34k profit. Clean slate.

### 🤯 Breakdown #2146: 300502.SZ on 2025-11-05 (Action: HOLD)

**Brief Rationale (What the bosses see):**
> Price sits just 8% above invalidation at 320.0 with weak trend structure (ADX 24.9, RSI 48.1 below 50, 60-min ZS in RANGE). 5-day return -14.77% confirms breakdown momentum. No technical confirmation of trend resumption despite price being above old invalidation. Risk/reward unfavorable: 8% downside to stop vs potential upside to 400 trim zone requires trend recovery that is not yet evident. Small position (29%) already reflects defensive posture. Wait for either (a) clean breakdown through 320, or (b) structural confirmation (ADX>30, RSI>50, 60-min ZS UP) before considering any action.

**Inner Journal (What she is actually screaming inside):**
> I bought at 395.59 on Oct 27, and now it's at 346.1. That's a -12.5% loss in 7 days. I was wrong - this wasn't a mature trend continuation, it was a blow-off top near 410.62. The 5-day -14.77% drop tells the real story: momentum has collapsed. ADX fell from strong to weak (24.9), RSI broke below 50, and the 60-min ZS Stack shifted to RANGE mode. These are breakdown signals, not pullback signals.

Now I'm trapped in a classic dilemma: do I cut losses at -12.5%, or hold and hope it recovers? My thesis says BREAKDOWN with invalidation at 320.0. Price is 346.1 - just 8% above my stop. If I sell now, I lock in a -12.5% loss. If I wait and it breaks 320, I lose -19%. The asymmetry is terrible.

I notice I'm avoiding the decision. HOLD feels safe because it's not a concrete action. But HOLD in a breakdown is just delayed SELL. The lack of 60-min BSP entry signal (none currently) gives me an excuse to wait. But Rule 6 says never ADD into pullback/breakdown, and Rule 7 says after 15 days with <2% return, I must TRIM. I'm at day 7 with -12.5% return, so Rule 7 hasn't triggered yet.

The real question: if I had no position, would I buy here at 346.1? No. The trend structure is broken. So why am I holding? Hoping. That's not a strategy.

But... 60-day return is still +89.7%. That was a massive trend. Maybe this is just a deep pullback before resumption? If it recovers above 320*1.03 = 329.6, Rule 2 says I should re-evaluate. But I'm already in the position. This rule is for re-entry after stop-loss, not for holding through.

I think I'm making a classic mistake: anchoring to my 395.59 entry price instead of seeing the current reality. The market doesn't care what I paid. The current price is 346.1, and the trend structure is weak. I should probably SELL and accept the loss. But I'm scared of selling near a potential bottom. This is emotional trading, not disciplined.

For now, I'll HOLD and watch the 320 level closely. If it breaks, I sell. If it holds and shows recovery signs (ADX up, RSI above 50, 60-min ZS turns UP), I'll reassess. But I'm not proud of this indecision. I'm holding because I can't accept being wrong so quickly after entry. That's ego, not investing.

Position at 56.5% with +5.9% return after 15 days. Holding until trend direction clarifies - either pullback rejection or continuation above MA5. Will ADD if momentum shifts back to acceleration/developing and price holds ¥440+.

Which signal do I trust? The structural (ADX 37.7, ZS UP, BSP T1买) or the tactical (trend_mode=pullback, RSI declining)?

I almost added today because the dip brought price right into my zone. But Rule 6 saved me - 'NEVER execute ADD if momentum.trend_mode is pullback'. Last time I ignored this rule, I added into a falling knife and regretted it.

The fact that I'm up 5.9% after 15 days is decent. Not amazing, but the thesis hasn't broken. I need to wait for the market to show its hand. If this is just a healthy pullback in an acceleration trend, price will hold above ¥430 and momentum will flip back to 'developing' or 'acceleration'. Then I can add aggressively.

My mistake would be adding here, then watching price dump through ¥430 and having to stop out. Better to miss a few points of upside than violate my own rules. Rules > feelings. I need to learn patience - let the structure confirm before committing more capital.

Observation: When ADX is >35 but trend_mode flips to pullback, it's often the transition between acceleration phases. The market is digesting the recent 31% 20-day gain. I should wait for digestion to complete.
